script.say: See if you can turn that same warmth inward now. The same patience you give so freely to others — let a little of it come home to you.
script.action: Turn the kindness inward. Then a long pause.
Silence — about 22 seconds. This is the emotional centre; give it room.

script.say: Your body has done remarkable things. It deserves your patience, not your criticism.
script.action: Echoes an existing affirmation almost verbatim. Slow delivery.
Silence — about 16 seconds.

script.say: You do not have to be finished to be enough. You are enough, in the unfinished middle of everything.
script.action: Affirmation in Vera's register. Let it rest.
Silence — about 18 seconds.

script.say: Now you can let the words go entirely. No more phrases to repeat. Just rest here, held by your own kindness and your own breath.
script.action: Release all instruction. Open the long restful dwell.

script.say: If your hand is resting on your heart or belly, feel it rise and fall. If not, just feel the breath moving, all on its own.
script.action: Re-offer both anchors equally. Then go quiet for a long while.
Long silence — about 75 seconds. This is the heart of the 15-minute length: open, unstructured rest. Do not fill it.

script.say: Wherever your mind wandered, that is okay. Wandering is not failing. Just gently come back, with the same kindness, to this quiet.
script.action: One soft re-anchoring, mirroring the existing 'wherever your mind wandered' line. Then quiet again.
Silence — about 55 seconds. Let the rest continue, undisturbed.
Continued silence — about 18 more seconds of open rest before the gentle return begins.

script.say: In a little while, we will begin to return. There is no hurry. Stay in the warmth a few breaths longer.
script.action: Signal the turn toward returning, softly. A few slow breaths.
Silence — about 18 seconds.

script.say: Now take one slightly deeper breath in, and let it out slowly. Feeling yourself here, in this room, on this day.
script.action: Begin reorienting. One deeper breath, exhale long.

script.say: Let a small amount of gentle movement come back. Maybe a soft wiggle of the fingers, or a slow roll of the shoulders. Only what feels good.
script.action: Optional gentle re-animating movement; keep it permission-based.
Silence — about 14 seconds, letting movement happen at the user's pace.

script.say: If your eyes have been closed, let them open whenever you are ready. No rush. Let the light back in slowly.
script.action: Re-orient to the room. Soft pacing.

script.say: See if you can carry one small piece of this kindness with you. Not as a task — just a softer way of speaking to yourself for the rest of today.
script.action: Bridge the practice into the day. Warm, no pressure.
Silence — about 12 seconds.

script.say: You showed up for yourself today, and that is its own kind of strength. Thank you for giving yourself this time.
script.action: Close in the sessionDone register. Sincere and quiet.

script.say: Whenever you are ready, the practice is complete. The breath, and the kindness, come with you.
script.action: Final line, echoing the existing close. Let it end softly into silence.
Closing silence to the 900-second mark. End gently, no chime required.

meditations.theme: Body scan — explicitly opt-in about body focus, trauma-aware. A slow, head-to-toe (or foot-to-head) journey of friendly attention, with a standing breath-and-sound alternative offered throughout for anyone for whom turning toward the body does not feel safe today.
meditations.intent: Offer a settling, non-striving body scan that rebuilds a kind, neutral relationship with the body at the practitioner's own pace. Every region is an invitation, never an instruction; attention can rest on breath or sound instead of the body at any moment with zero loss. The aim is gentle awareness and permission, not relaxation-on-demand or fixing anything.
meditations.scaleNote: This 20-minute library body scan is the long sibling of the shorter scans. The added time is spent almost entirely in silence and dwell, not in more talking: each region (feet, legs, pelvis/belly, back, hands/arms, shoulders/neck, face, whole body) gets its own unhurried pass with a long resting silence after the invitation, where the shorter versions group several regions per breath and move on quickly. The settling at the start (0-180s) and the resting integration near the end are both lengthened. Instruction density stays flat — the same number of spoken cues, simply spaced further apart by silence. The opt-out to breath or sound is repeated more often across the longer arc so a person can change their mind at any region.
meditations.accessibilityNotes: Every spoken segment doubles as an on-screen caption via the existing aria-live cue pairing, so the practice is fully usable with sound off or with a screen reader; nothing depends on hearing the voice. There is no avatar or visual movement to track — this is a captions-and-audio practice, so reduced-motion is honored by default and no pose rig is required. Body focus is opt-in: a non-body alternative (resting attention on the breath or on the sounds in the room) is offered at the start and repeated at each region, so a person can complete the entire practice without ever turning attention to the body. Eyes may stay open or closed throughout. Any position is welcome — lying, seated, or standing — and shifting position at any time is encouraged, not a failure. There is no timer pressure surfaced to the user and every pause is optional; a person may end early at any point and it still counts as showing up. Silences are intentional, not gaps to fill.
disclaimers: This practice is for general wellbeing and relaxation. It is not medical, psychological, or therapeutic advice, and it is not a treatment for any condition.

Turning attention toward the body can sometimes bring up strong feelings. If that happens, it is welcome to open your eyes, rest your attention on the breath or on sounds instead, or stop entirely. You are always in charge.

If you are experiencing significant distress, trauma symptoms, or thoughts of harming yourself, please reach out to a qualified professional or a local crisis or support line. This practice is not a substitute for that support.
Move only in ways that feel safe for your body today. If you are postpartum or have any medical concern, follow the guidance of your own healthcare provider.

script.say: Welcome. This is a body scan, and it is entirely yours to shape. There is nothing to fix here, and nothing to get right.
script.action: Warm, unhurried pace. Let the voice settle before continuing.

script.say: Find a position that feels kind. Lying down, sitting, or standing are all welcome. You can change it any time.
script.action: Allow time to settle into position.

script.say: Before we begin, one gentle note. In this practice we will turn attention toward the body, slowly and softly.
script.action: Spoken plainly, no pressure.

script.say: If turning toward the body does not feel right today, that is completely okay. You can rest your attention on your breath, or on the sounds around you, instead.
script.action: Offer the opt-out clearly and warmly.

script.say: Either path is the whole practice. Choose whichever feels more like care right now.
script.action: Silence — about ten seconds — to let the choice land.

script.say: Your eyes can close, or stay open with a soft gaze. Whatever helps you feel safe.
script.action: Gentle body cue: invite soft eyes, no insistence.

script.say: Let us begin by simply arriving. One easy breath in.
script.action: Breath pacing: slow inhale, roughly four seconds.

script.say: And a slow breath out, a little longer than the breath in.
script.action: Breath pacing: long exhale, roughly six seconds.

script.say: There is no special way to breathe. Just let it move on its own, and notice that it is happening.
script.action: Silence — about fifteen seconds — natural breathing.

script.say: Feel the support underneath you. The floor, the chair, the ground. It is holding you, and you do not have to do anything to deserve that.
script.action: Gentle body cue: notice contact and support.

script.say: Let yourself rest into being held for a moment.
script.action: Silence — about twenty-five seconds.

script.say: When you are ready, we will begin to travel slowly through the body, like a warm light moving at its own pace. Remember, breath or sound is always an option instead.
script.action: Bridge into the scan; re-offer the opt-out.

script.say: Let us start far away from the center, at the feet. If it feels okay, bring a soft, curious attention down to your feet.
script.action: Gentle body cue: invite attention to the feet.

script.say: Not searching for anything in particular. Just noticing whatever is there, or noticing nothing at all. Both are fine.
script.action: Silence — about thirty seconds — resting with the feet.

script.say: There is no right sensation to find. Warmth, coolness, tingling, or quiet. You are simply visiting, like saying a kind hello.
script.action: Silence — about twenty-five seconds.

script.say: Now, if you wish, let that soft attention drift up into the lower legs. Shins, calves, the backs of the knees.
script.action: Gentle body cue: invite attention up to the lower legs.

script.say: These legs carry you through your days. You do not need to thank them or change them. Just let them be here with you.
script.action: Silence — about thirty seconds — resting with the lower legs.

script.say: If your mind has wandered off, that is what minds do. Gently come back, without any scolding. Or stay with the breath, if that feels easier.
script.action: Permission-giving; re-offer the breath anchor.

script.say: Let the attention rise to the upper legs. Thighs, the large muscles, resting heavy and soft.
script.action: Gentle body cue: invite attention to the thighs. Silence after — about thirty seconds.

script.say: Nothing to hold, nothing to brace. If you notice gripping anywhere, see if it can soften, just a little. And if it does not, that is okay too.
script.action: Silence — about twenty-five seconds.

script.say: We are arriving now at the center of the body, the pelvis, the hips, the belly. This area can hold a lot of story. Move here only as gently as you like.
script.action: Trauma-aware framing for the center; slow, soft delivery.

script.say: If this region feels tender to visit, you are warmly invited to rest your attention on your breath instead, or on the sounds around you. You lose nothing by doing so.
script.action: Re-offer the opt-out explicitly at the most sensitive region.

script.say: If it feels okay, simply notice the breath rising and falling here, low and slow, with no agenda.
script.action: Silence — about thirty-five seconds — resting with the breath at the center, or wherever attention feels safe.

script.say: You are allowed to take up this space. You are allowed to rest here without earning it.
script.action: Silence — about thirty seconds.

script.say: Let the attention float now to the back. The lower back, then slowly up along the spine.
script.action: Gentle body cue: invite attention to the back.

script.say: So much is carried here, often without notice. You do not have to set anything down. Just let the back know it has your kind attention.
script.action: Silence — about thirty-five seconds — resting with the back.

script.say: And if at any point the silence feels like a lot, the breath is right there, steady, like a friend waiting.
script.action: Reassure about the silence; offer breath anchor.

script.say: Let us visit the hands. They are often busy. Let them be still now, palms open or resting, however is comfortable.
script.action: Gentle body cue: invite attention to the hands. Silence after — about thirty seconds.

script.say: Now up through the arms. The wrists, the forearms, the upper arms. Heavy and at ease.
script.action: Gentle body cue: invite attention to the arms. Silence after — about thirty seconds.

script.say: Nothing to reach for. Nothing to carry. Just arms, resting, off duty for these few minutes.
script.action: Silence — about thirty seconds.

script.say: Bring a soft attention to the shoulders and the neck. A place that quietly holds the weight of long days.
script.action: Gentle body cue: invite attention to shoulders and neck.

script.say: On your next breath out, you might let the shoulders drop a little, away from the ears. Or simply notice them, exactly as they are.
script.action: Breath pacing: one slow exhale. Then silence — about thirty seconds.

script.say: Let the attention rise gently to the face. The jaw, often clenched without our knowing. Let it be a little loose.
script.action: Gentle body cue: invite attention to the jaw and face.

script.say: The space between the eyebrows, softening. The eyes, resting back in their sockets. The whole face, unguarded, with no one to perform for.
script.action: Silence — about thirty-five seconds — resting with the face.

script.say: And now, if it feels welcome, let attention widen to hold the whole body at once. From the feet all the way to the crown of the head.
script.action: Gentle body cue: widen attention to the whole body.

script.say: One body, breathing, resting, here. You do not have to scan or search any more. Just be with all of it, softly.
script.action: Silence — about forty seconds — whole-body rest.

script.say: Or, if you have stayed with the breath or the sounds this whole time, rest there now, just as whole and just as welcome.
script.action: Silence — about thirty-five seconds. Honor the alternative path equally.

script.say: Let yourself simply rest. Nothing to do. Nowhere to be. This is enough. You are enough, exactly as you are right now.
script.action: Silence — about forty seconds — open resting.

script.say: In a moment we will come back. There is no hurry. Let the breath become a touch fuller, if it wants to.
script.action: Breath pacing: a slightly fuller breath, unforced.

script.say: If your eyes have been closed, let them open when they are ready, taking in the room slowly.
script.action: Gentle body cue: soft return, eyes opening at their own pace.

script.say: If it feels right, rest a hand on your heart, and let this land. You gave yourself this time. That matters.
script.action: Gentle body cue: hands to heart, matching the existing kind close. Optional.

script.say: You showed up for yourself today, and that is its own kind of strength. Carry a little of this softness with you.
script.action: Affirmation in Vera voice; warm close.

script.say: Whenever you are ready, the practice is complete. Be gentle with yourself out there.
script.action: Final words, then silence to the end at 1200s.
